segments.text: When all the kings of the Amorites, who were beyond the Jordan westward, and all the kings of the Canaanites, who were by the sea, heard how Yahweh had dried up the waters of the Jordan from before the children of Israel until we had crossed over, their heart melted, and there was no more spirit in them, because of the children of Israel.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: At that time, Yahweh said to Joshua,
segments.speaker: The king of Jericho

segments.text: Make flint knives, and circumcise again the sons of Israel the second time.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Joshua made himself flint knives, and circumcised the sons of Israel at the hill of the foreskins.
segments.speaker: The king of Jericho

segments.text: This is the reason Joshua circumcised them: all the people who came out of Egypt, who were males, even all the men of war, died in the wilderness along the way, after they came out of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: For all the people who came out were circumcised; but all the people who were born in the wilderness along the way as they came out of Egypt had not been circumcised.
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: For the children of Israel walked forty years in the wilderness until all the nation, even the men of war who came out of Egypt, were consumed, because they didn’t listen to Yahweh’s voice. Yahweh swore to them that he wouldn’t let them see the land which Yahweh swore to their fathers that he would give us, a land flowing with milk and honey.
Their children, whom he raised up in their place, were circumcised by Joshua, for they were uncircumcised, because they had not circumcised them on the way.
When they were done circumcising the whole nation, they stayed in their places in the camp until they were healed.
Yahweh said to Joshua,
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from you.
segments.speaker: Joshua

segments.text: Therefore the name of that place was called Gilgal to this day.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The children of Israel encamped in Gilgal. They kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the month at evening in the plains of Jericho.
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: They ate unleavened cakes and parched grain of the produce of the land on the next day after the Passover, in the same day.
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: The manna ceased on the next day, after they had eaten of the produce of the land. The children of Israel didn’t have manna any more, but they ate of the fruit of the land of Canaan that year.
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: When Joshua was by Jericho, he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, a man stood in front of him with his sword drawn in his hand. Joshua went to him and said to him,
segments.speaker: Rahab

segments.text: Are you for us, or for our enemies?
segments.speaker: Joshua

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: The men

segments.text: No; but I have come now as commander of Yahweh’s army.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Joshua fell on his face to the earth, and worshiped, and asked him,
What does my lord say to his servant?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The prince of Yahweh’s army said to Joshua,
Take off your sandals, for the place on which you st and is holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Joshua did so.
segments.speaker: The prince of Yahweh’s army
